name: Gorontalo

sources.author: Steinhauer, Hein
sources.title: Sentence-initial elements in Gorontalo
sources.notes: Descriptive source on Gorontalo; a starting point for a future grapheme map.

notes: Gorontalo (Bahasa Hulontalo) — Philippine-type Austronesian language of northern Sulawesi, Indonesia; written in the Latin script today (historically also in Jawi/Arabic script). THE GRAPHEME INVENTORY IS NOT YET SPECIFIED here (graphemes/allophones are empty). The phoneme inventory is known — five vowels /a e i o u/ plus nasals /m n ɲ ŋ/, plosives /p t d̠ c k ʔ b d ɟ ɡ/, the implosives /ɓ ɗ/ and sonorants /w r j h l/ — but no published, citable letter→IPA table exists for the Latin orthography, in particular for how the implosives and the palatal series are written, so no map is claimed rather than guessing an Indonesian-style convention.

ancestors.notes: Austronesian — the classification node this language sits under.
